title: Yoga-based intervention for carpal tunnel syndrome: a randomized trial
headline: Yoga-based intervention for carpal tunnel syndrome: a randomized trial
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined sleep. The yoga group had significantly more improvement in Phalen sign (12 improved vs 2 in control group; P = .008), but no significant differences were found in sleep disturbance, Tinel sign, and median nerve motor and sensory conduction time.
keyFindings: The yoga group had significantly more improvement in Phalen sign (12 improved vs 2 in control group; P = .008), but no significant differences were found in sleep disturbance, Tinel sign, and median nerve motor and sensory conduction time.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Yoga-based intervention for carpal tunnel syndrome: a randomized trial
aiSnippet: RCT, JAMA (1998). The yoga group had significantly more improvement in Phalen sign (12 improved vs 2 in control group; P = .008), but no significant differences were found in sleep disturbance, Tinel sign, and median nerve motor and sensory conduction time.
